meta.description: Maps frontier AI/ML topics to course phases, related lessons, and classification rules. Used to score and route frontier signals.

scoringModel.description: relevance_score = lesson_match(40%) + weak_topic_match(25%) + source_credibility(15%) + implementation_value(10%) + commuter_usefulness(10%)

classificationRules.act_now: Directly reinforces current Month 1 lesson or weak topic. Score >= 70.
classificationRules.monitor: Relevant but belongs to Month 2+. Score 40–69. Add to LATER.md.
classificationRules.parked: Important topic but would distract from foundations. Score 20–39. Explicitly defer.
classificationRules.ignore: Hype, no implementation value, irrelevant to 3-year arc. Score < 20.

mappings.why_relevant_now: Core Week 4 content. Every signal about linear algebra directly reinforces current study.
mappings.implementation_path: Implement from scratch in NumPy — verify against sklearn
mappings.future_connection: Foundation for embeddings, attention mechanisms, PCA, gradient descent

mappings.why_relevant_now: Core Week 5 content. Bayesian reasoning is foundational for ML understanding.
mappings.implementation_path: Implement Bayes theorem, Gaussian, and MLE from scratch
mappings.future_connection: Foundation for ML model assumptions, uncertainty quantification, training

mappings.why_relevant_now: Python is the implementation language for everything. Any Python tooling improvement is immediately useful.
mappings.implementation_path: Implement mathematical operations using NumPy, plot with matplotlib
mappings.future_connection: All future projects built in Python

mappings.why_relevant_now: APIs are Week 3 core content and the interface layer for all LLM work.
mappings.implementation_path: Build REST client in Python, call Claude API
mappings.future_connection: Every LLM application is built on top of API patterns

mappings.why_relevant_now: Ollama is the Week 6 local LLM tool. Install and basic usage is current-phase content.
mappings.implementation_path: Install Ollama, run a model, call via Python requests
mappings.future_connection: Foundation for local agent execution, cost-free experimentation

mappings.why_relevant_now: Git is Week 2 content and the professional engineering habit underpinning everything.
mappings.implementation_path: Daily commits, branch management, meaningful commit messages

mappings.why_relevant_now: Math foundations are being built now. Monitor embedding concepts to contextualize the linear algebra.
mappings.future_connection: Core primitive for RAG, semantic search, recommendation systems

mappings.why_relevant_now: Calculus and linear algebra foundations being built now enable understanding gradient descent in Month 2.
mappings.implementation_path: Implement gradient descent from scratch on a toy problem
mappings.future_connection: Foundation for all ML training — neural networks, fine-tuning

mappings.why_relevant_now: Build awareness now, act later. Requires embeddings + backprop + neural network foundations first.
mappings.commuter_note: Conceptual overview via audio is fine — do not attempt implementation until Month 3+
mappings.implementation_path: Implement scaled dot-product attention from scratch after Month 3 neural network foundations
mappings.future_connection: Core architecture of every modern LLM

mappings.why_parked: Requires embeddings (Month 2), vector DBs (Month 3), and LLM API patterns (Month 2–3) as prerequisites.
mappings.implementation_path: PDF → chunk → embed → ChromaDB → similarity search → Claude context injection
mappings.future_connection: Core architecture for personal knowledge assistant and AI engineering portfolio

mappings.why_parked: Requires embeddings and RAG understanding first. Premature without Month 2 foundations.
mappings.implementation_path: ChromaDB local → Pinecone cloud → production retrieval patterns
mappings.future_connection: Storage layer for all RAG and semantic search systems

mappings.why_parked: Requires LLM API mastery, RAG understanding, and Python architecture patterns. Premature in Month 1.
mappings.implementation_path: Tool use API → ReAct pattern → multi-step agent → evaluation framework
mappings.future_connection: The destination — production AI agents are the capstone of this curriculum

mappings.why_parked: MCP requires solid agent foundations. Defer until agents phase is reached.
mappings.implementation_path: Build an MCP server → connect to Claude Desktop → build compound agent

mappings.why_relevant_now: REST API patterns are Week 3 — LLM APIs extend the same pattern. Light awareness is fine now.
mappings.implementation_path: Claude API wrapper → streaming → conversation history → multi-turn
mappings.future_connection: Interface layer for all LLM engineering work

mappings.why_parked: Requires deep understanding of transformers, backprop, and gradient descent. Premature in Month 1–2.
mappings.implementation_path: LoRA fine-tune small model → evaluate → deploy
mappings.future_connection: Customization layer for production AI systems

mappings.why_parked: Evaluation requires having something to evaluate. Build first, then measure.
mappings.implementation_path: Build eval dataset → run RAGAS → iterate on RAG pipeline quality
mappings.future_connection: Quality gate for all production AI systems

mappings.why_parked: Research-level content. Interesting for context but not actionable in Month 1–3.
mappings.commuter_note: Conceptual awareness OK via audio — no implementation planned
mappings.implementation_path: N/A — understand for general LLM engineering context
mappings.future_connection: Informs model selection and infrastructure decisions

mappings.why_relevant_now: API patterns (Week 3) naturally lead to prompt engineering. Light practice is useful now.
mappings.implementation_path: Build prompt template library → test with Claude API → iterate
mappings.future_connection: Core skill for all LLM engineering — the first applied layer
